tweets.text:
my read of the fable situation:

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

similar to how epic games intentionally broke app store rules in 2020, forcing apple to ban fortnite, affecting 100 million ios players

thread_tweets.text:
my read of the fable situation:

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

similar to how epic games intentionally broke app store rules in 2020, forcing apple to ban fortnite, affecting 100 million ios players
thread_tweets.full_text:
my read of the fable situation:

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

similar to how epic games intentionally broke app store rules in 2020, forcing apple to ban fortnite, affecting 100 million ios players

thread_tweets.text:
similarly in 2020 epic gradually escalated against apple for months until it finally decided to flat-out defy the most important app store rule, tricking apple's review process and inserting a third-party payment option into the live app

epic knew full well they'll get banned for this, which is why they prepared an entire video ad campaign ahead of time to publicly kickstart their fight against apple

anthropic's recent behavior reads exactly the same way
thread_tweets.full_text:

thread_tweets.text: @TimSweeneyEpic to be clear i’m glad epic fought this battle and won, but that’s an aside and i wanted this post to be about anthropic 😂
thread_tweets.full_text: @TimSweeneyEpic to be clear i’m glad epic fought this battle and won, but that’s an aside and i wanted this post to be about anthropic 😂

tweets.full_text:
my read of the fable situation:

anthropic decided to take its own customers hostage to improve its negotiating position vs the government

similar to how epic games intentionally broke app store rules in 2020, forcing apple to ban fortnite, affecting 100 million ios players

similarly in 2020 epic gradually escalated against apple for months until it finally decided to flat-out defy the most important app store rule, tricking apple's review process and inserting a third-party payment option into the live app

epic knew full well they'll get banned for this, which is why they prepared an entire video ad campaign ahead of time to publicly kickstart their fight against apple

anthropic's recent behavior reads exactly the same way

@TimSweeneyEpic to be clear i’m glad epic fought this battle and won, but that’s an aside and i wanted this post to be about anthropic 😂

tweets.text:
Not sure what feels better right now:

1) Having been one of the very few VCs that backed @Collector_Crypt early in 2024, when all the other tIeR oNe VCs passed because they thought it was too niche to ever return venture like outcomes, and now it's one of the few winning verticals in crypto.

2) Having been one of the very few liquid investors who literally doubled down at the very bottom, when everyone declared $CARDS dead while the underlying fundamentals said the complete opposite.

I usually don't like to brag about our wins like this. But finding one of the very few big winners this market has produced in years, then playing it perfectly while everyone around you calls you an idiot, feels damn good ngl.

It's exactly these kinds of winners and the privilege of working with such talented teams that I'm here for.

And why I will never, ever give up.

Higher, for longer.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Doubling down on our $CARDS bags here on the liquid market.

@Collector_Crypt consistently ranges between $25-35M in annual gross profit, while the token FDV is currently hovering around $60M, which I largely attribute to tough market conditions and a complete lack of interest in https://t.co/Mx9FBSaNmD

tweets.text: Sinclair's new preprint provides the first direct experimental evidence for the Information Theory of Aging: your cells don't age because they break — they age because they forget who they are. The biological blueprint is still intact. The cells just stop reading it correctly. That distinction changes the entire target: you don't repair damage. You restore data. And data can be restored.
tweets.full_text: Sinclair's new preprint provides the first direct experimental evidence for the Information Theory of Aging: your cells don't age because they break — they age because they forget who they are. The biological blueprint is still intact. The cells just stop reading it correctly. That distinction changes the entire target: you don't repair damage. You restore data. And data can be restored.
